subject: eddy-lazzarin

sectors_excited.name: Zero-knowledge proofs (ZK scaling + privacy)
sectors_excited.why: ZK is the substrate for both scaling (validity-proof rollups, zkEVMs) and privacy-preserving applications. Decades-old theory, but the practical research curve has bent sharply because of web3 incentives. This is foundational infrastructure, not a fad, and I'm actively contributing (KZG ceremony) — still on the steep part of the sigmoid.

citations.quote: Although the field has fascinating decades-old roots, research has accelerated radically as a result of web3.
I just contributed to the 🕯️ KZG Ceremony 🕯️ using lazzarin.eth to help scale Ethereum

sectors_excited.name: Fully on-chain games
sectors_excited.why: Games are serious incentive systems, not a hobby. Open economies, player-owned assets, and composable, autonomous worlds (EVE proved many of these principles pre-blockchain). Development cycles are long, so we're early — but the niche the great engineer-games once filled will be filled on-chain.

citations.quote: I believe fully on-chain games will fill a similar niche in coming years.

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized social (Farcaster, XMTP)
sectors_excited.why: We learned the hard way that building on centralized social platforms gets you rugged. Neutral, user-owned protocols with a healthy client ecosystem are the antidote — interoperable messaging and a sufficiently-decentralized social graph you can build on permissionlessly.

citations.quote: Farcaster is the neutral platform we wanted all along.
The Farcaster ecosystem is growing, with already a dozen interfaces and clients, and many tools

sectors_excited.name: AI + crypto (autonomous, self-owning programs)
sectors_excited.why: AI is how machines think; crypto is how machines own. Together you get programs that can hold assets and act for themselves. We only have ~8-14 years of thought around sovereign autonomous applications, so this is the rawest, most exciting design frontier right now.

citations.quote: crypto = how machines can own
An autonomous program on a crypto network that can fund and grow itself.

sectors_excited.name: Decentralized identity / unbundled auth
sectors_excited.why: Everyone now has a public/private keypair plus a neutral data store — that lets us replace username/password auth with self-sovereign identity (Sign-in with Ethereum, DIDs, verifiable credentials), making identity more private, secure, and programmable for web2 and web3 alike.

citations.quote: make identity more private, secure, and programmable
With DIDs, we can use the public keys generated by crypto wallets in more contexts, potentially replacing username/password authentication and giving people complete control of their data.

sectors_concerned.name: Opaque centralized exchanges / custodians
sectors_concerned.why: Post-FTX, the 'proof of reserves' theater is the tell. A Merkle tree with no shared code, no liability treatment, and no proof of address control is useless and self-exploitable. Cryptography favors the defender — self-custody and real on-chain verification beat trusting a black-box custodian.

citations.quote: Binance's system is useless and easily exploited by them.

sectors_concerned.name: Token-first projects / 'tokenomics' consulting
sectors_concerned.why: A token is a tool, not a goal. Projects that lead with a pie chart and allocations instead of designing a protocol around a clear goal, constraints, and mechanisms are doing the wrong 5% first. I expect most of these to fail on contact with reality.

citations.quote: A made up pie chart is not real work.
A protocol needs a clear goal. Launching a token isn't a goal.

sectors_concerned.name: Platform gatekeeping (Apple's 30% / mobile)
sectors_concerned.why: The biggest tax on crypto-native innovation isn't a token — it's the closed mobile platform. Apple will cripple, block, or skim any native crypto experience, which is why mobile UX in crypto is so poor. The browser remains the most important invention for a free and open web, and the escape hatch.

citations.quote: The effects of Apple's 30% fee on innovation are radically underestimated
Apple will cripple you, block you, or take your lunch if you try to offer a native experience.

tokens_excited.name: ETH
tokens_excited.why: The clear leader by developer mindshare and fee demand, and the Merge to proof-of-stake went off cleanly — a roadmap years in the making, executed. ZK rollups becoming the predominant compute layer is the long-planned next step. I've been optimistic on Ethereum's fundamentals for years and remain so.

citations.quote: Looks smooth so far. Unbelievable. Congratulations and well done to the ETH 2 researchers, client teams, core devs, and all other contributors.
In one year, Ethereum has grown from settling ~$373m per day to over $9b per day.

tokens_excited.name: MKR (Maker)
tokens_excited.why: My standing exemplar of real token design: MKR is a stakeholding/risk token that underwrites the protocol — shortfalls dilute holders, good stewardship accrues value — with genuine, on-chain-measurable fundamentals. The point isn't a price call; it's that the design is coherent and earns its token, which is rare.

citations.quote: the role of MKR and AAVE/COMP as the risk token that underwrites the protocol often seems to take a back seat rhetorically to "governance" unfortunately
If you look at the top DAOs, most have fundamentals now.

tokens_concerned.name: BNB / Binance
tokens_concerned.why: My concern is the issuer's opacity, not a chart. The 'proof of reserves' they shared has a known fatal flaw around negative balances and proves no control of the addresses included. An asset whose backstop is an unverifiable centralized black box carries counterparty risk I can't reason about.

citations.quote: Binance's system is useless and easily exploited by them.

risk_regime.why: I fly by instruments, not sentiment. The price-innovation cycle says builders advance the tech right through winter, and prices are a lagging surface over that progress — despite financial turbulence, things are proceeding according to plan. Skeptical in analysis, optimistic in action; it's still early. This is a constructive, build-through-the-trough posture, not a trading signal.

citations.quote: the right balance is to be skeptical in analysis and optimistic in action
despite financial turbulence, things are proceeding according to plan
It's still early.

notes: I think in protocols and fundamentals, not price targets — tokens are the native asset class of information networks, and the interesting question is always the mechanism, not the pie chart. We're mid fourth price-innovation cycle, post-FTX; the noise is sentiment, the signal is where the talent and code are going (ZK, on-chain games, decentralized social/identity, and now AI+crypto). Disclosures apply; none of this is investment advice.
